mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production indicate expanding economic capacity, which supports higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns during periods of INDPRO growth (monthly increase > 0.1%) are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, it signals labor market weakness and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative corporate earnings expectations and downward pressure on equities.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, the median 5-day forward SPX return is positive or zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and weakness in housing starts indicates an unsustainable consumption-led expansion not supported by residential investment, leading to a correction in equity prices.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 1% and HOUST month-over-month change < -2%
failure_condition: Average 20-day SPX return following the trigger condition is positive or less than -0.5% with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During recessionary periods (real GDP declining quarter-over-quarter), a reduction in the federal funds rate provides monetary stimulus that is particularly effective in boosting equity valuations, leading to positive forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreases by at least 25 basis points over the past month
failure_condition: During recessionary periods when FEDFUNDS decreases, 20-day SPX returns are not significantly positive (mean < 1%, t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation exceeds 3% year-over-year, it erodes consumer purchasing power and raises the cost of capital, leading to compressed equity valuations and negative returns.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%, the average 20-day SPX return is positive or not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment increases strongly while industrial production declines, it signals a divergence between the service sector and manufacturing, indicating an unbalanced economic expansion that is likely to reverse, producing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 200K and INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5%
failure_condition: The average 20-day SPX return following the joint trigger condition exceeds 0% or is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth is the fundamental driver of corporate earnings. The long-term upward trend in GDPC1 supports a secular positive drift in equity prices over multi-quarter horizons.
failure_condition: If over any 5-year window, the 60-day SPX return is not positively correlated with GDPC1 growth (correlation ≤ 0).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts indicates a sudden contraction in residential investment, which is a leading indicator of economic weakness and typically leads to negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If the median 5-day SPX return after a >10% monthly decline in HOUST is positive or the hit rate (negative returns) is less than 60%.
